title: ConfigMap Hot Reload via Volume Mount
description:
Deploy nginx with a custom config file served from a ConfigMap volume mount. Because it is volume-mounted (not env var), updating the ConfigMap automatically updates the file inside the running pod within ~60 seconds — no pod restart needed.

**Common Pitfalls:** Only volume-mounted ConfigMaps hot-reload. Env var injections (envFrom/valueFrom) never update in a running pod. Also note: nginx itself must be signaled to reload its config — file update alone does not restart nginx's worker processes.
steps: Create a ConfigMap 'nginx-custom-conf' with key 'default.conf' containing a simple nginx server block on port 8080
Create a Deployment 'nginx-hot' with 1 replica mounting the ConfigMap at /etc/nginx/conf.d/
Verify the pod is Running and the mounted file shows the correct port
Edit the ConfigMap to change the port to 9090
Wait ~60s and exec into the pod to verify the file now contains 9090
hints: The nginx.conf server block uses 'listen <port>;' to set the port
Use kubectl exec <pod> -- cat /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf to inspect the mounted file
kubectl edit configmap nginx-custom-conf lets you update the ConfigMap in-place
Volume-mounted ConfigMap files update automatically — the kubelet handles this
Mount the nginx-conf volume at '/etc/nginx/conf.d/' so nginx loads the custom server block

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: nginx-custom-conf
data:
  default.conf: | # TODO: Add nginx server block listening on port 8080
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-hot
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx-hot
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx-hot
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:alpine
        volumeMounts:
        - name: nginx-conf
          mountPath: # TODO: use '/etc/nginx/conf.d/'
      volumes:
      - name: nginx-conf
        configMap:
          name: nginx-custom-conf

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: nginx-custom-conf
data:
  default.conf: |
    server {
      listen 8080;
      location / {
        return 200 'hello from nginx-hot\n';
      }
    }
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-hot
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx-hot
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx-hot
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:alpine
        volumeMounts:
        - name: nginx-conf
          mountPath: /etc/nginx/conf.d/
      volumes:
      - name: nginx-conf
        configMap:
          name: nginx-custom-conf
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo 'Checking ConfigMap nginx-custom-conf exists...'
if ! kubectl get configmap nginx-custom-conf &>/dev/null; then
  echo '✗ ConfigMap nginx-custom-conf not found'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ ConfigMap nginx-custom-conf exists'

echo 'Checking default.conf key contains listen directive...'
CONF=$(kubectl get configmap nginx-custom-conf -o jsonpath='{.data.default\.conf}')
if ! echo "$CONF" | grep -q 'listen'; then
  echo '✗ default.conf missing listen directive'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ default.conf has listen directive'

echo 'Checking Deployment nginx-hot exists...'
if ! kubectl get deployment nginx-hot &>/dev/null; then
  echo '✗ Deployment nginx-hot not found'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ Deployment nginx-hot exists'

echo 'Waiting for Deployment to be available...'
kubectl rollout status deployment/nginx-hot --timeout=90s
echo '✓ Deployment nginx-hot is available'

echo 'Verifying ConfigMap is volume-mounted at /etc/nginx/conf.d/...'
POD=$(kubectl get pod -l app=nginx-hot -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
if ! kubectl exec "$POD" -- ls /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf &>/dev/null; then
  echo '✗ /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf not found in pod'
  exit 1
fi
echo '✓ default.conf mounted in pod'

echo ''
echo 'All checks passed!'
exit 0
